page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs, consisting of 8 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and INT8 operations. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a compute-to-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core capabilities, with single-threaded gzip compression performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core scores rank in the top 25% tier. Multi-core and memory-bound operations generally fall within the middle 50% average tier, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations. For machine learning workloads, the hardware achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M LLM models, though prompt processing and medium 7B model inference remain in the average tier.

As a member of the c8i-flex family, this server offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-heavy workloads by utilizing a balanced resource profile without local storage overhead. The primary tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and a modest baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, which may limit data-intensive clustering. The server is appropriate for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz, 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a 480 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. In benchmark testing, it demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The server represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is appropriate for software compilation, text processing, web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and high-speed DDR5 memory for processing-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX support
Top-tier single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks
No local storage and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Appropriate for software compilation, web serving, and small LLM inference
